  "title": "Experienced Native Android Developer",
  "description": "UWA - The Pan-African Social Network - We are young entrepreneurs seeking to give Africa and the world a social network / social hub that captures Afrocentric expression, culture, and way of life.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Experienced Native Android Developer

Job Location: Lagos, Nigeria

Description


	Are you an Experienced Native Android Developer or Experienced Native iOS Developer based in Nigeria? This is your opportunity to join a team building innovative digital solutions and make an impact.
	If you&rsquo;re passionate about creating high-quality mobile applications and ready for your next challenge, we&rsquo;d love to hear from you.


Qualification


	Candidates should possess relevant qualifications


Why Join UWA Social


	Make impact across Africa
	Growth &amp; Innovation
	Collaborative Culture


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV/Resume to: Fred@uwasocial.com using the Job Position as the subject of your email.",

Experienced Native Android Developer Job at UWA - the Pan-African Social Network




        
    

        
        UWA - The Pan-African Social Network - We are young entrepreneurs seeking to give Africa and the world a social network / social hub that captures Afrocentric expression, culture, and way of life.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Experienced Native Android Developer

Job Location: Lagos, Nigeria

Description
     

            
    




	Are you an Experienced Native Android Developer or Experienced Native iOS Developer based in Nigeria? This is your opportunity to join a team building innovative digital solutions and make an impact.
	If you’re passionate about creating high-quality mobile applications and ready for your next challenge, we’d love to hear from you.


Qualification


	Candidates should possess relevant qualifications


Why Join UWA Social


	Make impact across Africa
	Growth & Innovation
	Collaborative Culture


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV/Resume to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of your email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Executive Assistant",
  "description": "Pishon and Brooks Advisory Services is a Professional Services firm that support our clients by managing their end-to-end HR Processes in order to enhance their productivity. At PBAS, we have the expertise to connect and manage every business process within the employee lifecycle.

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Executive Assistant&nbsp;

Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Job Summary&nbsp;


	The Executive Assistant will provide high-level administrative and strategic support to the CEO / Managing Director, ensuring the smooth and efficient operation of the executive office.
	This role demands exceptional organizational ability, discretion, and a proactive approach to managing priorities in a fast-paced business environment.


Key Responsibilities
Executive Support:


	Manage the CEO&#39;s calendar, scheduling meetings, appointments, and travel arrangements with precision.
	Prepare and review correspondence, reports, presentations, and briefing materials on behalf of the CEO.
	Act as the first point of contact for the CEO&#39;s office &mdash; screening calls, emails, and visitors.
	Coordinate board meetings, management meetings, and executive retreats including agenda preparation and minute-taking.
	Follow up on action items and decisions emanating from executive meetings.


Administrative &amp; Office Coordination:


	Manage travel logistics including flight bookings, hotel reservations, and itinerary planning for local and international trips.
	Maintain a well-organized filing system for executive correspondence and confidential documents.
	Liaise with internal departments, external partners, and stakeholders on behalf of the CEO.
	Handle procurement of office supplies and manage expenses related to the executive office.


Strategic &amp; Project Support:


	Conduct research and compile data to support executive decision-making and business initiatives.
	Monitor and track key projects, deadlines, and deliverables to keep the CEO informed.
	Support the preparation of business proposals, strategic documents, and company presentations.
	Maintain confidentiality of sensitive business information at all times.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Business Administration, Secretarial Studies, Mass Communication, or a related field.
	Minimum of 5 years of experience as an Executive Assistant or Senior Personal Assistant, preferably supporting C-suite executives.
	Proven track record of managing complex schedules, multiple priorities, and confidential information.
	Experience in publishing, education, retail, or a related industry is an advantage.


Required Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Exceptional organizational and time management skills with strong attention to detail.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills; professional and articulate.
	High proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook).
	Ability to handle sensitive matters with utmost discretion, diplomacy and confidentiality.
	Strong interpersonal skills with a polished, professional presence.
	Proactive, self-motivated, and capable of working with minimal supervision.
	Ability to multitask, prioritizes under pressure, and meets tight deadlines.
	Ability to set up and handle virtual meetings platform.
	Excellent in data management and record keeping.
	Possess emotional intelligence and integrity.
	(Male Preferred).
",
  "datePosted": "2026-07-14",
  "validThrough": "2026-08-15",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Pishon and Brooks Advisory Services"

Executive Assistant Job at Pishon and Brooks Advisory Services




        
    

        
        Pishon and Brooks Advisory Services is a Professional Services firm that support our clients by managing their end-to-end HR Processes in order to enhance their productivity. At PBAS, we have the expertise to connect and manage every business process within the employee lifecycle.

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Executive Assistant 

Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Job Summary 
     

            
    




	The Executive Assistant will provide high-level administrative and strategic support to the CEO / Managing Director, ensuring the smooth and efficient operation of the executive office.
	This role demands exceptional organizational ability, discretion, and a proactive approach to managing priorities in a fast-paced business environment.


Key Responsibilities
Executive Support:


	Manage the CEO's calendar, scheduling meetings, appointments, and travel arrangements with precision.
	Prepare and review correspondence, reports, presentations, and briefing materials on behalf of the CEO.
	Act as the first point of contact for the CEO's office — screening calls, emails, and visitors.
	Coordinate board meetings, management meetings, and executive retreats including agenda preparation and minute-taking.
	Follow up on action items and decisions emanating from executive meetings.


Administrative & Office Coordination:


	Manage travel logistics including flight bookings, hotel reservations, and itinerary planning for local and international trips.
	Maintain a well-organized filing system for executive correspondence and confidential documents.
	Liaise with internal departments, external partners, and stakeholders on behalf of the CEO.
	Handle procurement of office supplies and manage expenses related to the executive office.


Strategic & Project Support:


	Conduct research and compile data to support executive decision-making and business initiatives.
	Monitor and track key projects, deadlines, and deliverables to keep the CEO informed.
	Support the preparation of business proposals, strategic documents, and company presentations.
	Maintain confidentiality of sensitive business information at all times.


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor's Degree in Business Administration, Secretarial Studies, Mass Communication, or a related field.
	Minimum of 5 years of experience as an Executive Assistant or Senior Personal Assistant, preferably supporting C-suite executives.
	Proven track record of managing complex schedules, multiple priorities, and confidential information.
	Experience in publishing, education, retail, or a related industry is an advantage.


Required Skills & Competencies:


	Exceptional organizational and time management skills with strong attention to detail.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills; professional and articulate.
	High proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook).
	Ability to handle sensitive matters with utmost discretion, diplomacy and confidentiality.
	Strong interpersonal skills with a polished, professional presence.
	Proactive, self-motivated, and capable of working with minimal supervision.
	Ability to multitask, prioritizes under pressure, and meets tight deadlines.
	Ability to set up and handle virtual meetings platform.
	Excellent in data management and record keeping.
	Possess emotional intelligence and integrity.
	(Male Preferred).
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Graphic Artist",
  "description": "Brands Optimal Limited is one of Africa&rsquo;s leading experiential marketing agencies, which optimizes value for clients through the use of strategic, reliable, and measurable methods in consumer engagement, loyalty seeding, and sales generation.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Graphic Artist

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos

Job Summary


	The Graphic Artist will be responsible for creating visually compelling designs that communicate our clients&#39; brand messages across print, digital, and experiential marketing platforms.
	The ideal candidate should possess excellent design skills, creativity, and the ability to work in a fast-paced environment while meeting tight deadlines.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop creative concepts and high-quality visual designs for marketing campaigns.
	Design digital and print materials, including social media content, flyers, brochures, banners, presentations, and branding assets.
	Collaborate with the Creative, Strategy, and Client Service teams to deliver impactful campaigns.
	Prepare artwork for production and ensure brand consistency across all projects.
	Stay up to date with design trends, tools, and industry best practices.
	Manage multiple projects simultaneously while meeting deadlines.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s degree, HND, or OND in Graphic Design, Fine Arts, Visual Communication, or a related field.
	Minimum of 2 years of experience in a similar role, preferably within an advertising, branding, or marketing agency.
	Proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign, and CorelDRAW). Knowledge of After Effects or Premiere Pro is an added advantage.
	Strong creative portfolio showcasing design skills.
	Excellent communication, time management, and attention to detail.
	Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team environment.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary.
	Career growth and professional development opportunities.
	A collaborative, innovative, and creative work environment.
	Opportunity to work on exciting brands and impactful campaigns.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to: jobs@brandsoptimal.com using&nbsp; the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Graphic Artist Job at Brands Optimal Limited




        
    

        
        Brands Optimal Limited is one of Africa’s leading experiential marketing agencies, which optimizes value for clients through the use of strategic, reliable, and measurable methods in consumer engagement, loyalty seeding, and sales generation.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Graphic Artist

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos

Job Summary
     

            
    




	The Graphic Artist will be responsible for creating visually compelling designs that communicate our clients' brand messages across print, digital, and experiential marketing platforms.
	The ideal candidate should possess excellent design skills, creativity, and the ability to work in a fast-paced environment while meeting tight deadlines.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop creative concepts and high-quality visual designs for marketing campaigns.
	Design digital and print materials, including social media content, flyers, brochures, banners, presentations, and branding assets.
	Collaborate with the Creative, Strategy, and Client Service teams to deliver impactful campaigns.
	Prepare artwork for production and ensure brand consistency across all projects.
	Stay up to date with design trends, tools, and industry best practices.
	Manage multiple projects simultaneously while meeting deadlines.


Requirements


	Bachelor's degree, HND, or OND in Graphic Design, Fine Arts, Visual Communication, or a related field.
	Minimum of 2 years of experience in a similar role, preferably within an advertising, branding, or marketing agency.
	Proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign, and CorelDRAW). Knowledge of After Effects or Premiere Pro is an added advantage.
	Strong creative portfolio showcasing design skills.
	Excellent communication, time management, and attention to detail.
	Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team environment.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary.
	Career growth and professional development opportunities.
	A collaborative, innovative, and creative work environment.
	Opportunity to work on exciting brands and impactful campaigns.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to: [email protected] using  the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Assistant Editor, BBC Hausa",
  "description": "The BBC is an international multimedia broadcaster on radio, TV, online and social networks with a weekly global audience of 320 million. As part of an historic multi-million pound expansion of BBC Africa, the BBC World Service is introducing a wide range of new and exciting language and regional content to serve African and global audiences.

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Assistant Editor, BBC Hausa&nbsp;

Job Requisition ID: 48977
Job Location: Abuja
Employment Type: Full-time

Purpose of the Role


	BBC World Service is evolving its editorial team in Abuja while future-proofing its journalism to meet changing media consumption habits across sub-Saharan Africa.&nbsp;
	BBC Hausa is one of the most trusted international news providers for audiences across West Africa and a highly engaged global diaspora. With a strong editorial heritage, the service is entering an exciting new phase: transforming into a digital-first, video-led operation designed to reach a new generation of Hausa audiences who primarily consume news via social media on smartphones.&nbsp;


Key Responsibilities and Impact


	Lead BBC Hausa&rsquo;s transformation into a digital-first, video-led service, embedding a culture of high editorial ambition, personal accountability, mutual respect and continuous learning.&nbsp;
	Commission, shape and deliver high-impact original journalism, including exclusives, investigations, explainers as distinctive digital first content.&nbsp;
	&nbsp;Work closely with the editorial leadership team in Nigeria to identify shared editorial priorities, coordinate coverage of cross-service stories to drive efficiency and minimise duplication.&nbsp;
	Develop and implement a data-led video strategy, driving measurable digital growth with a clear focus on increasing reach among younger and female audiences.&nbsp;
	Use audience insight, analytics and platform trends to shape commissioning, distribution and editorial decision-making, ensuring content remains competitive and relevant.&nbsp;
	Embed a culture of performance awareness within the team, supporting journalists to understand what drives engagement and how to use data to improve their output.&nbsp;
	Oversee the effective use of AI tools within editorial workflows, improving efficiency in areas such as research, translation, transcription, editing and optimisation while maintaining full editorial oversight.&nbsp;


Your Skills and Experience
Essential Criteria:


	Proven experience managing and developing journalism teams, with the ability to build a positive, high-performing culture and support individuals to deliver their best work.&nbsp;
	Strong track record of commissioning, shaping and delivering high-impact digital journalism, with a clear understanding of what drives audience engagement.&nbsp;
	Demonstrable success in developing and implementing data-led video strategies, delivering measurable audience growth and impact.&nbsp;
	Advanced understanding of audience analytics, search optimisation and platform performance, with the ability to translate data into effective editorial decisions.&nbsp;
	Fluency in written and spoken Hausa, with a strong grasp of tone, nuance and audience expectations.&nbsp;


Why Join the Team


	This Assistant Editor role is central to that transformation. The successful candidate will help build BBC Hausa&rsquo;s video and visual storytelling capability, contributing to filming, editing, scripting, presenting, and end-to-end content production.
	This is a role for an editor who combines strong journalistic instincts with genuine digital ambition; someone who can lead a team through a significant transformation in how BBC Hausa reaches and serves its audiences, while maintaining the editorial standards and audience trust that make the service matter.&nbsp;

Assistant Editor, BBC Hausa Job at BBC Nigeria




        
    

        
        The BBC is an international multimedia broadcaster on radio, TV, online and social networks with a weekly global audience of 320 million. As part of an historic multi-million pound expansion of BBC Africa, the BBC World Service is introducing a wide range of new and exciting language and regional content to serve African and global audiences.

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Assistant Editor, BBC Hausa 

Job Requisition ID: 48977
Job Location: Abuja
Employment Type: Full-time

Purpose of the Role
     

            
    




	BBC World Service is evolving its editorial team in Abuja while future-proofing its journalism to meet changing media consumption habits across sub-Saharan Africa. 
	BBC Hausa is one of the most trusted international news providers for audiences across West Africa and a highly engaged global diaspora. With a strong editorial heritage, the service is entering an exciting new phase: transforming into a digital-first, video-led operation designed to reach a new generation of Hausa audiences who primarily consume news via social media on smartphones. 


Key Responsibilities and Impact


	Lead BBC Hausa’s transformation into a digital-first, video-led service, embedding a culture of high editorial ambition, personal accountability, mutual respect and continuous learning. 
	Commission, shape and deliver high-impact original journalism, including exclusives, investigations, explainers as distinctive digital first content. 
	 Work closely with the editorial leadership team in Nigeria to identify shared editorial priorities, coordinate coverage of cross-service stories to drive efficiency and minimise duplication. 
	Develop and implement a data-led video strategy, driving measurable digital growth with a clear focus on increasing reach among younger and female audiences. 
	Use audience insight, analytics and platform trends to shape commissioning, distribution and editorial decision-making, ensuring content remains competitive and relevant. 
	Embed a culture of performance awareness within the team, supporting journalists to understand what drives engagement and how to use data to improve their output. 
	Oversee the effective use of AI tools within editorial workflows, improving efficiency in areas such as research, translation, transcription, editing and optimisation while maintaining full editorial oversight. 


Your Skills and Experience
Essential Criteria:


	Proven experience managing and developing journalism teams, with the ability to build a positive, high-performing culture and support individuals to deliver their best work. 
	Strong track record of commissioning, shaping and delivering high-impact digital journalism, with a clear understanding of what drives audience engagement. 
	Demonstrable success in developing and implementing data-led video strategies, delivering measurable audience growth and impact. 
	Advanced understanding of audience analytics, search optimisation and platform performance, with the ability to translate data into effective editorial decisions. 
	Fluency in written and spoken Hausa, with a strong grasp of tone, nuance and audience expectations. 


Why Join the Team


	This Assistant Editor role is central to that transformation. The successful candidate will help build BBC Hausa’s video and visual storytelling capability, contributing to filming, editing, scripting, presenting, and end-to-end content production.
	This is a role for an editor who combines strong journalistic instincts with genuine digital ambition; someone who can lead a team through a significant transformation in how BBC Hausa reaches and serves its audiences, while maintaining the editorial standards and audience trust that make the service matter.
